trade.summary: Solid bilateral trade. Italy exports machinery, chemicals, fashion, and food; Mexico exports auto parts, electronics, and agricultural goods. Italian companies are active in Mexico's manufacturing sector. The EU-Mexico trade agreement modernization has supported expanded trade flows.

diplomatic.summary: Italy and Mexico maintain good diplomatic relations bolstered by a large Italian diaspora community in Mexico and strong cultural affinities. Both are G20 members. Italy is an important EU voice in Latin American relations. Periodic high-level visits. Mexico values Italian support within EU multilateral contexts.

military.summary: Limited military ties. Italy is NATO member; Mexico maintains a non-aligned, domestically focused military posture. Some defense equipment sales. No formal alliance.

financial.summary: Meaningful bilateral investment. Italian companies in manufacturing, energy (ENI has Mexico operations), and financial services. Mexican investment in Italian market is limited. EU-Mexico economic partnership framework supports financial ties.

energy.summary: ENI has significant oil and gas operations in Mexico following Mexico's energy sector opening. Bilateral energy cooperation is meaningful, with Italian energy companies engaged in exploration and production.

scientific.summary: Active scientific cooperation through EU-Mexico science programs. Joint research in archaeology, earth sciences, and biomedical fields. Italian universities have exchange programs with leading Mexican institutions.
